question.text: Standalone Primary Dealers (SPDs) are permitted to participate in currency futures markets. What is the minimum Net Owned Fund required for an SPD to be allowed to participate in currency futures?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states: "Standalone Primary Dealers (SPD) having a minimum Net Owned Fund of ₹250 crore or any amount as prescribed for undertaking diversified activity shall be allowed to participate in currency futures." **The Trap:** Rs. 500 crores is the net worth threshold for AD Category-I banks. Rs. 100 crores is the exchange net worth requirement. Rs. 250 crores is uniquely linked to SPDs. **Cross-Exam Context:** SPDs may participate as clients or direct TMs/CMs but can only trade on their own account — not on behalf of clients. This restricts them further than bank TMs. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SPD minimum = Rs. 250 Cr; Bank TM/CM minimum = Rs. 500 Cr. SPDs trade only proprietarily, never for clients.
